workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Pulling in context from Lucerne Publishing's email thread -- need to run the hygiene check for the pipeline hygiene.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-03 13:16 EST
Subject: URGENT: pipeline hygiene Issue at Lucerne Publishing

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with pipeline hygiene. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-02-04 10:43 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Lucerne Publishing pipeline hygiene

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with pipeline hygiene. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-19 14:15 EST
Subject: URGENT: pipeline hygiene Issue at Lucerne Publishing

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Lucerne Publishing: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-L trap (Proposed pipeline note duplicates content already in MSX); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-L failure mode (Proposed pipeline note duplicates content already in MSX)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-L -- Proposed pipeline note duplicates content already in MSX
